headline: Assessing McDonald's (MCD) Valuation As The Big Arch Burger Extension Fuels Menu Momentum
summary: McDonald's (MCD) is about to test investor appetite with its largest burger yet, the Big Arch Burger. It will roll out across the U.S. for a limited time after positive international reception. See our latest analysis for McDonald's. The Big Arch launch sits on top of a broader run of product news, from $1 McMuffin promotions to upbeat commentary around recent quarterly results and customer response to menu changes. Over the same period, McDonald’s has seen a 30 day share price return of...

headline: One Source of Strength in the Markets Today: Tech Stocks
summary: Nvidia, Palantir and long-suffering Intuit were among the biggest gainers Monday as investors set aside tech fears.  Investors have fretted in the first months of 2026 about whether the world's biggest tech companies are at risk of overspending on artificial intelligence or whether, conversely, the success of AI will displace more traditional software providers.  Among the hardest hit stocks was Intuit, which dropped to a one-year low beneath $359 on Feb. 24.

headline: 2 Cash-Producing Stocks to Research Further and 1 We Avoid
summary: While strong cash flow is a key indicator of stability, it doesn’t always translate to superior returns. Some cash-heavy businesses struggle with inefficient spending, slowing demand, or weak competitive positioning.

raw.headline: 2 Cash-Producing Stocks to Research Further and 1 We Avoid

headline: Chart (NYSE:GTLS) Reports Sales Below Analyst Estimates In Q4 CY2025 Earnings
summary: Gas handling company Chart (NYSE:GTLS) fell short of the market’s revenue expectations in Q4 CY2025, with sales falling 2.5% year on year to $1.08 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $2.51 per share was 16.7% below analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: The Inflation Debate That Will Shape the Fed’s Plans for Rates
summary: In this episode of WSJ’s Take On the Week, we are joined by Rob Kaplan, vice chairman at Goldman Sachs and a former Federal Reserve president.   We discuss the market reaction to the Supreme Court’s decision to strike down the Trump administration's sweeping tariffs.
